root_motivation: Give any MCP-capable agent a read-only, structured view of the live session population (census + verdict) without exposing the reap kill path — so agents can query 'what sessions are alive' through a connector rather than shelling pgrep.
user_persona: Joe Yen, running wintermute on Arch Linux; self-review and build-tick agents connect via MCP connector and need session population data without manual subprocess plumbing.

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo build --release produces muster-mcp; cargo test green; MSRV 1.85, no let-chains; sigpipe::reset() first in main().
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac1.rs — build succeeds, test suite green

acceptance_criteria.description: muster-mcp serve completes initialize and tools/list returns exactly the two read tools (sessions_census, sessions_verdict) with valid JSON-Schema inputSchema.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac2.rs — serve roundtrip with initialize + tools/list

acceptance_criteria.description: Read-only by construction: a test asserts the backend verb set ⊆ {census, verdict} and a guard rejects 'reap' appearing as a subcommand literal anywhere in command construction.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac3.rs — verb allowlist + reap absent check

acceptance_criteria.description: sessions_verdict invokes 'muster verdict --format json' and returns the parsed JSON (verified against a stubbed muster binary on PATH; offline).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac4.rs — stub muster binary, check verdict output

acceptance_criteria.description: A tools/call naming an unregistered tool (e.g. sessions_reap) yields a JSON-RPC error, not a shell call — proving reap is unreachable.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac5.rs — unknown tool call returns -32601 error

acceptance_criteria.description: --muster-bin/MUSTER_BIN redirects to a fixture script; absent a usable binary, serve starts and tool calls return a clean ToolError.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac6.rs — env override + missing binary graceful error

acceptance_criteria.description: README documents the two tools, that reap is intentionally excluded, and an MCP client config snippet; no settings.json edits performed.
acceptance_criteria.test: Manual: README.md contains reap exclusion mention and config snippet

scope: Binary crate muster-mcp serving two MCP tools: sessions_census and sessions_verdict
Backend shells the installed muster binary with allowlisted verb literals + --format json
JSON-RPC 2.0 stdio MCP server via mcp-core
non_goals: Exposing muster reap over MCP — intentionally excluded
Implementing muster logic directly (shells the binary)

five_whys_trace.a: Agents (self-review, build-tick) currently guess at session population via pgrep — they need structured, attributable census+verdict data.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not call muster directly from agent code?
five_whys_trace.a: Agents run through MCP connectors; a real MCP server means any connector-aware agent can query without per-agent subprocess plumbing.

five_whys_trace.q: Why exclude reap from the MCP surface?
five_whys_trace.a: Reap kills sessions; MCP here is an inspection surface. Keeping kill off the connector is a safety invariant — an agent can see but never act.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should sessions_census and sessions_verdict accept parameters (e.g. filters)?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Both are no-arg per the PRD — inputSchema is an empty object. Parameters are a follow-on PRD concern.
